back_translation: On that day, the Book of Moses was read in the hearing of the people, and in it was found written that no Ammonite or Moabite should enter the assembly of God forever,
because they had not come out to meet the Israelites with food and water, but had hired Balaam to curse Israel — though our God turned the curse into a blessing.
As soon as the people heard this law, they separated all foreigners from Israel.
Before this, Eliashib the priest, who was a relative of Tobiah, had been put in charge of the storerooms of the house of our God.

He had prepared for Tobiah a large room where formerly were stored the grain offerings, the frankincense, the temple vessels, and the tithes of grain, new wine, and oil — the portions commanded for the Levites, singers, and gatekeepers, and the contributions for the priests.
During all this I was not in Jerusalem, because in the thirty-second year of Artaxerxes king of Babylon, I had returned to the king. Some time later, I requested leave from the king
and came to Jerusalem. There I discovered the evil that Eliashib had done for Tobiah by preparing for him a room in the courts of the house of God.
I was greatly displeased, and I threw all of Tobiah's household goods out of the room.
Then I ordered the rooms to be purified, and I had the vessels of the house of God, the grain offerings, and the frankincense brought back into them.
I also learned that the portions of the Levites had not been given to them, and so the Levites and singers who did the work had fled, each to his own field.
So I rebuked the officials and asked, "Why has the house of God been neglected?" Then I gathered them and stationed them at their posts.
And all Judah brought the tithes of grain, new wine, and oil into the storehouses.

I appointed Shelemiah the priest, Zadok the scribe, and Pedaiah from the Levites over the storehouses, and with them Hanan son of Zaccur, son of Mattaniah, as assistant, because they were considered trustworthy. Their duty was to distribute portions to their brothers.
O my God, remember me for this, and do not blot out the deeds of steadfast love that I have done for the house of my God and for its services.

In those days I saw people in Judah treading wine presses on the Sabbath, bringing heaps of grain and loading them onto donkeys — along with wine, grapes, figs, and every kind of load — and bringing them into Jerusalem on the Sabbath day. So I warned them about selling food on that day.
Moreover, men of Tyre living in the city brought in fish and all kinds of merchandise, and sold them on the Sabbath to the people of Judah in Jerusalem.
Then I rebuked the nobles of Judah and said to them, "What evil is this that you are doing — profaning the Sabbath day?
Did not your fathers do the same, so that our God brought all this calamity upon us and upon this city? Now you are adding to His wrath against Israel by profaning the Sabbath!"

When the shadows fell on the gates of Jerusalem just before the Sabbath, I ordered the doors to be shut, and ordered that they not be opened until after the Sabbath. I posted some of my servants at the gates so that no load could enter on the Sabbath day.
Once or twice, the merchants and sellers of all kinds of goods spent the night outside Jerusalem.
But I warned them and said, "Why are you spending the night by the wall? If you do this again, I will lay hands on you." From that time on, they did not come on the Sabbath again.

Then I commanded the Levites to purify themselves and come to guard the gates, to keep the Sabbath day holy. O my God, remember me for this also, and have mercy on me according to the abundance of Your steadfast love.
Also in those days, I saw Jews who had married women from Ashdod, Ammon, and Moab.
Half of their children spoke the language of Ashdod or the languages of other peoples, and could not speak the language of Judah.

So I rebuked them and cursed them, and beat some of these men and pulled out their hair. I made them take an oath before God, saying, "You shall not give your daughters in marriage to their sons, nor take their daughters as wives for your sons or for yourselves.

Did not Solomon king of Israel sin in such matters? Among many nations there was no king like him, and he was loved by his God, who made him king over all Israel. Yet foreign women caused even him to sin.
Shall we now hear that you also are doing all this great evil, acting unfaithfully against our God by marrying foreign women?"
One of the sons of Jehoiada son of Eliashib the high priest had become a son-in-law to Sanballat the Horonite. Therefore I drove him away from me.
Remember them, O my God, because they defiled the priesthood and the covenant of the priesthood and the Levites.
So I purified them from everything foreign, and I assigned duties to the priests and the Levites, each in his own task.
And I arranged for the wood offering at appointed times, and for the firstfruits. O my God, remember me for good.
